activities_projects.title: Analysis of Labour Laws
activities_projects.objective: Understand how laws protect workers from exploitation
steps: Read the section about labour laws in the chapter
Identify two laws that protect workers
Explain how these laws improve working conditions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyses the role of laws in protecting workers

activities_projects.title: Case Study: Minimum Wage Law
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of minimum wage laws
steps: Imagine a situation where workers are paid very low wages
Explain how minimum wage laws help them
Describe what might happen if such laws did not exist
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies legal concepts to real-life situations

activities_projects.title: Debate on Corporate Responsibility
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the role of companies in ensuring social justice
steps: Organise a class debate on the topic 'Companies should be responsible for worker safety'
Prepare arguments supporting the statement
Write a summary of the main points discussed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical thinking on corporate responsibility
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments and summary (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparing Worker Rights
activities_projects.objective: Understand different rights given to workers
steps: Write two rights workers should have in workplaces
Explain why these rights are important
Compare these rights with situations where workers may be exploited

activities_projects.title: Research on a Workplace Safety Law
activities_projects.objective: Understand laws that ensure safety in workplaces
steps: Find a law related to workplace safety
Write what the law requires employers to do
Explain how it protects workers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the importance of workplace safety laws

activities_projects.title: Role of Government in Social Justice
activities_projects.objective: Understand how government ensures fairness in society
steps: Read the section about government responsibility
Write two ways the government protects workers and consumers
Explain why enforcement of laws is necessary
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyses the role of government in ensuring justice

activities_projects.title: Mock Court on Worker Rights
activities_projects.objective: Understand how legal systems solve disputes
steps: Create a scenario where workers are treated unfairly
Assign roles such as judge, worker and employer
Discuss how the law would solve the problem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies legal concepts to workplace situations

activities_projects.title: Comparing Social Justice in Different Sectors
activities_projects.objective: Understand how laws affect workers in different industries
steps: Choose two sectors such as factories and construction
Write problems workers may face in each sector
Explain how laws can improve their conditions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyses the need for labour laws in different sectors

activities_projects.title: Critical Thinking: Society Without Labour Laws
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the consequences of lack of labour protection
steps: Imagine a society without labour laws
Write three problems workers might face
Explain how labour laws help prevent these problems
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical thinking about labour protection

activities_projects.title: Essay on Law and Social Justice
activities_projects.objective: Summarise the importance of laws in promoting justice
steps: Write an essay explaining how laws promote social justice
Include examples such as minimum wage and workplace safety
Explain why government enforcement is important
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesises understanding of law and social justice
